era_context: Late Old Kingdom Egypt (Dynasty VI or V); Early Dynastic III Mesopotamia; Indus Valley Civilisation in its mature phase. Writing systems are well-established in river valleys. Most of the world remains preliterate.
geographic_coverage_gaps: Northern Europe and the Steppe (sparse archaeological evidence)
East Asia beyond the Yellow River (limited documentation)
disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: The historical record for -3094 is essentially non-existent. No specific events are documented for this year. The period is known only through king lists and general archaeological evidence. Chronological uncertainty is substantial. The assignment of this year to any specific historical period is based on conventional chronology, which is disputed by scholars using alternative chronological systems.
